title: Sorted Two Sum (Array Foundations)
steps: Set left pointer at array start
Set right pointer at array end
If sum is target, return indices
If sum < target, move left pointer right
If sum > target, move right pointer left
Repeat until pointers cross, then return not found
explanation: Two pointers work because the array is sorted. Move the pointer that helps the sum get closer to the target.
intuition: One pointer from each side lets you adjust the sum quickly without checking all pairs.

tokens.explanation: The loop starts from the left side with the left pointer.
It must compare left against right to know when pointers cross.
Left pointer is the lower bound.
Right pointer is the upper bound.

title: Container With Most Water
steps: Move the pointer with smaller height
explanation: Moving the shorter side is the only way to possibly increase area, because width always shrinks.
intuition: You keep the chance of finding a taller wall by discarding the shorter one.

python.code: while __0__ < __1__:\n    width = __1__ - __0__\n    area = min(h[left], h[right]) * width

tokens.explanation: Left pointer starts as lower bound.
Right pointer starts as upper bound.

title: Valid Parentheses
steps: If opening bracket, push to stack
If closing bracket, stack must not be empty
Pop top and verify it matches closing bracket
At end, return stack is empty
explanation: The stack stores the most recent opening bracket, so each closing bracket can be matched in reverse order.
intuition: Last opening bracket should close first. Stack naturally models that behavior.

tokens.explanation: Opening brackets are pushed to remember them.
A closing bracket compares with the latest opening bracket.
Read top before removing or comparing.
Peek checks the latest opening bracket.

title: Classic Binary Search
steps: If target is larger, move left to mid + 1
If target is smaller, move right to mid - 1
explanation: Binary search halves the search space every step, which makes it fast on sorted arrays.
intuition: Each comparison tells you which half cannot contain the target.

python.code: while __0__ <= __1__:\n    mid = (__0__ + __1__) // 2\n    if nums[mid] == target: return mid
